L1.area: Sandbox runtime internals and object isolation infrastructure

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where a proxy handler object, leaked to sandboxed code through a debugging inspection path, can be directly invoked with a forged target argument. By supplying an arbitrary host object as the target to the handler's prototype-chain traversal trap, an attacker can walk host prototype chains, extract cross-realm symbols, and reach host intrinsics that enable arbitrary code execution on the host process.

L3.area: Proxy handler trap methods within the sandbox's inter-realm bridging layer
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where a proxy handler object, leaked to sandboxed code via Node.js util.inspect with the showProxy option enabled, can be directly invoked with an attacker-forged target argument rather than the proxy's legitimate internal target. By supplying a host built-in object as the forged target to the handler's prototype-walking trap, an attacker traverses host prototype chains to extract cross-realm symbols that ultimately enable constructing and executing arbitrary code on the host process, achieving full sandbox escape with remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: BaseHandler.getPrototypeOf and other proxy trap methods in the sandbox bridge accept a target parameter without validating it against the handler's registered proxy target, allowing leaked handlers to be invoked with attacker-forged targets to walk host prototype chains and escape the sandbox for arbitrary code execution.
